summary:
I have successfully identified the type-coercion-comparison bug ("the needle") and fixed it.

The issue was that `query.minRating` in `utils.js` was being extracted as a string rather than a number. In `filter.js`, the loop `for (let i = min; i <= max; i++)` would push the initial string value into the `range` array, and subsequent values as integers due to the `i++` coercion. When checking if `p.rating` was in the acceptable range using `acceptable.includes()`, it failed for the initial string value because `includes()` uses strict equality (`===`).

I corrected this by wrapping the assignment in `parseInt(..., 10)` within `utils.js`, ensuring it is stored as a number. Running `test.sh` now confirms all test cases pass successfully.
